shared_constraints: Preserve the original castle, floating-island world, watercolor storybook style, palette, camera, perspective, and wide framing.
Every road must be a continuous warm cream dirt path supported by a broad grassy floating-island landmass with visible rock cliffs, trees, shrubs, and waterfalls.
A road meeting a side socket must reach the canvas edge exactly at that socket, be perpendicular to the edge for its final 8 percent, and remain unobstructed.
Do not create beige decorative patches that can be mistaken for roads. No bridges made only of road surface and no unsupported floating road ribbons.
No text, labels, arrows, signs, characters, vehicles, UI, borders, or watermark.

variants.label: Straight main route
variants.topology: Keep one continuous main road from B0 to the castle at T0, with a natural near-straight perspective route and no forks.

variants.label: Left sweep
variants.topology: Keep one continuous main road from B0 to T0; make it sweep broadly left in the foreground and ease back to the castle.

variants.label: Right sweep
variants.topology: Keep one continuous main road from B0 to T0; make it sweep broadly right in the foreground and ease back to the castle.

variants.label: Left-right S curve
variants.topology: Keep one main road from B0 to T0 with a readable S curve: first left, then right, then into the castle.

variants.label: Right-left S curve
variants.topology: Keep one main road from B0 to T0 with a readable reverse S curve: first right, then left, then into the castle.

variants.label: Near left exit
variants.topology: Add one unmistakable Y junction in the near foreground. The main road continues to T0; a supported left branch reaches L0 and exits the canvas.

variants.label: Near right exit
variants.topology: Add one unmistakable Y junction in the near foreground. The main road continues to T0; a supported right branch reaches R0 and exits the canvas.

variants.label: Near exits both sides
variants.topology: Create a clear four-way hub in the near foreground: B0 enters, the main road continues to T0, and supported branches exit through both L0 and R0.

variants.label: Middle left exit
variants.topology: Add one Y junction halfway to the castle. The main road continues to T0; a supported branch curves left to L1.

variants.label: Middle right exit
variants.topology: Add one Y junction halfway to the castle. The main road continues to T0; a supported branch curves right to R1.

variants.label: Far left exit
variants.topology: Add one small but unmistakable distant Y junction near the castle approach. Keep the main road to T0 and route a supported left branch to L2.

variants.label: Far right exit
variants.topology: Add one small but unmistakable distant Y junction near the castle approach. Keep the main road to T0 and route a supported right branch to R2.

variants.label: Near left loop
variants.topology: Split the road near B0 into a left scenic branch and a main branch; reconnect them cleanly before the midpoint, forming one grounded teardrop loop.

variants.label: Near right loop
variants.topology: Split the road near B0 into a right scenic branch and a main branch; reconnect them cleanly before the midpoint, forming one grounded teardrop loop.

variants.label: Middle left loop
variants.topology: At mid-distance split off a left route and reconnect it farther toward the castle, forming a clear compact loop on one coherent island.

variants.label: Middle right loop
variants.topology: At mid-distance split off a right route and reconnect it farther toward the castle, forming a clear compact loop on one coherent island.

variants.label: Diamond split and rejoin
variants.topology: Split the main road into equally readable left and right alternatives around a central rocky grove, then merge both routes back into one road to T0.

variants.label: Two sequential loops
variants.topology: Create two natural sequential split-and-rejoin choices: a broad left loop in the foreground followed by a smaller right loop in the middle distance.

variants.label: Left ingress merges
variants.topology: A supported road enters from L0 and merges smoothly into the B0-to-T0 main road at a clear near-foreground Y junction.

variants.label: Right ingress merges
variants.topology: A supported road enters from R0 and merges smoothly into the B0-to-T0 main road at a clear near-foreground Y junction.

variants.label: Left-right cross route
variants.topology: A supported L0-to-R0 route crosses and joins the main B0-to-T0 road at one clear central four-way intersection.

variants.label: Near-left and far-right exits
variants.topology: Place two distinct junctions: a near left branch exits at L0, then a distant right branch exits at R2; keep the main route continuous to T0.

variants.label: Far left-right cross route
variants.topology: A distant supported L2-to-R2 cross-route meets the main B0-to-T0 road before the castle.

variants.label: Cross route plus distant loop
variants.topology: A supported L0-to-R0 cross-route meets the main road, followed by one small split-and-rejoin loop before T0.

variants.label: Mid connector hub with rejoin
variants.topology: A broad mid-distance hub serves L1 and R1 while an internal right alternative rejoins the B0-to-T0 main road before the castle.
